seoTitle: Paradise vs Parker Range — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Paradise and Parker Range. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Paradise vs Parker Range: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Paradise and Parker Range.
heading: Paradise vs Parker Range — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Paradise, Western Australia and Parker Range, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Paradise has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Parker Range (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Paradise or Parker Range safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Paradise has a flood risk score of 30/100, while Parker Range scores 60/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Paradise scores 46/100 for bushfire risk. Parker Range scores 48/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Paradise or Parker Range?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Paradise has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.8% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
